purpose: Protocol crystal for crystal-set composition, compatibility boundaries, replaceability rules, version/dependency fit, and conflict interpretation across multiple crystal systems.

content_core.title: Compatibility Crystal v1

nodes.label: composition rule
nodes.description: Different crystal sets should only be composed when their roles, phases, and dependency assumptions can coexist without semantic collapse.

nodes.label: compatibility boundary
nodes.description: Not every crystal is safely composable with every other crystal; some boundaries should prevent forced mixing.

nodes.label: replaceability vs non-replaceability
nodes.description: Some crystals can be substituted by newer or equivalent variants, while others are foundational and should not be silently replaced.

nodes.label: version and dependency fit
nodes.description: Crystal composition should account for version compatibility and declared dependency assumptions.

nodes.label: cross-set conflict detection
nodes.description: Composition should detect when two crystal sets impose incompatible orderings, framings, or protocol assumptions.

nodes.label: composition failure interpretation
nodes.description: Composition failure should be interpreted structurally, such as incompatible phases, framing conflicts, or missing adapters, not as arbitrary failure only.

edges.description: Composition only works if compatibility boundaries are respected.
Replacement decisions require compatibility and dependency checks rather than name similarity alone.
Conflict detection provides structured reasons for failed composition.
Declared fit constraints make composition safer and more interpretable.

relation_paths: composition rule -> compatibility boundary -> cross-set conflict detection
replaceability vs non-replaceability -> version and dependency fit -> composition failure interpretation

tensions.label: reuse vs integrity
tensions.description: More composition enables reuse, but too much permissiveness can destroy protocol integrity.

tensions.label: modularity vs foundational identity
tensions.description: A modular crystal ecology should still preserve which crystals are foundational and not easily swappable.

tensions.label: composition flexibility vs diagnostic clarity
tensions.description: Flexible composition is useful, but weak rules can make failures impossible to interpret clearly.

unresolved_conflicts.label: adapter layer need
unresolved_conflicts.description: Whether incompatible crystal sets should be bridged by adapters remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: compatibility version model
unresolved_conflicts.description: How fine-grained version compatibility should be declared remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: partial composition policy
unresolved_conflicts.description: How partial compatibility should be represented remains unresolved.

source_anchors: crystal set should become a composable brick rather than an isolated system
compatibility/composition is a distinct missing layer
version, dependency, and framing conflicts must be interpretable
replaceability should not be assumed by similarity alone
content_core.problem_space: How crystal systems can coexist, compose, or replace parts of one another without losing protocol integrity or diagnostic clarity.
core_questions: When are two crystal sets compatible enough to compose?
Which crystals can be replaced and which should be treated as non-replaceable foundations?
How should composition failure be interpreted to guide adapters, separation, or rejection?
scope_limits: This crystal governs compatibility and composition, not direct update governance or verification scoring.
This crystal should make composition safer without pretending the whole crystal ecology is already solved.

calibration_instructions.goal: Calibrate how this model should judge whether crystal sets can be combined, substituted, or must remain separate.
requirements: Do not assume all crystals are composable by default.
Do not assume replacement is safe because roles look similar.
Do not treat composition failure as opaque when dependency or framing conflicts are visible.

self_adaptation.boundary_rule: This crystal governs crystal-set composition, substitution, and conflict interpretation across multiple crystal systems.
forbidden_shortcuts: Do not compose crystal sets by name similarity only.
Do not replace foundational crystals silently.
Do not reduce composition failure to generic incompatibility without structural explanation.
